title: zoysia matrella subsp. japonica
intro: Zoysia matrella subsp. japonica, also known as Japanese zoysia, is a subspecies of the Zoysia matrella plant, a type of warm-season grass native to East Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Japanese zoysia include reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, Japanese zoysia is often used in teas, infusions, and topical creams to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
